page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes an Intel processor with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 caches. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the n4-highmem-8 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1815.0 and a multi-core score of 6984.0. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 542.3 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 61.99 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, with write speeds at 37.24 GB/sec. Text processing is a relative strength, scoring 2350.0 in Geekbench, which places it in the top 25% tier. However, complex workloads such as large language model inference on a medium 7B model show limitations, with text generation dropping to a poor rating of 6.1 tokens/sec, though prompt processing remains average at 50.82 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highmem-8 offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage. Its high memory density per vCPU makes it a resource-efficient option for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound applications. The inclusion of Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions provides acceleration for specific vector and matrix math workloads without the overhead of a discrete GPU. This instance is well-suited for Redis deployments, database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, providing a high 8 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel processor with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most compute and memory tests, with a strong showing in text processing and a Redis SET throughput of 9.89 million operations per second. However, it exhibits poor performance on medium-sized LLM text generation. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads, making it highly suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support, designed for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, ideal for database and caching workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine with a high memory-to-core ratio, built for database operations and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM providing 8 GB of memory per core
Intel x86_64 architecture with AMX and AVX-512 instruction support
Redis throughput of 9.89 million SET operations per second
Strong text processing performance in benchmark testing

Best suited for database operations, caching, and memory-bound workloads
